mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in residential housing starts indicate a synchronized demand-supply shock that compresses corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 50% of historical co-contraction episodes, or the combined feature importance drops below 0.1 in a gradient boosting model.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment where the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, any month-over-month increase in real consumer credit outstanding accelerates liquidity inflows into equities, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The positive return differential between high-rate/credit-expansion periods and other regimes falls below 0.5% per month, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarterly increase in real GDP growth rate reflects expanding aggregate demand that directly supports corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth turns positive but SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of observations, breaking the causal link.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200 thousand additions in a single month while the unemployment rate simultaneously falls below 5.0%, it confirms a robust labor market that sustains consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual-threshold condition fails to produce a positive risk premium exceeding 1.0% over 20 days in at least 50% of historical instances, or forward returns show no statistical difference from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation where CPI exceeds 205, a month-over-month rise in industrial production signals supply-side resilience that mitigates stagflation fears, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production gains fail to lift equity valuations when inflation is elevated, or the forward return spread between high-inflation/production-growth and high-inflation/production-decline regimes is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls demonstrates continuous labor market absorption that underpins wage growth and consumption, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth consistently coincides with negative or flat 20-day equity returns, or the rolling 1-year Rank IC drops below 0.02 across multiple market cycles.
